Now I need to understand: when viewing a past turn, how should `pgRenderBoard`, `pgRenderPlayers`, `pgRenderBank` etc. use the snapshot? Let me look at the snapshot fields vs what these renderers read:

The snapshot stores `players`, `bank`, `bankDevCards`, `settlements`, `roads`, `robberHex`, plus `currentTurnIdx`, `eventLogLen`, `rollLen`. The renderers read directly from `pg.*`. So we need to swap in snapshot state for viewing.

Approach: when not viewing latest, temporarily restore `pg` state from snapshot (preserving the "live" state separately), render, then restore. Actually a cleaner approach: maintain a "live" backup and switch `pg` working state.

Looking at the design more carefully: `pg.turns` stores snapshots taken at each `pgSnapshotTurn`. The slider's `viewTurn` indexes into `pg.turns`. The latest turn snapshot might be stale (not include current pending actions) since the next snapshot is only pushed on end-turn.

The cleanest solution: introduce a "live" backup. When `viewTurn` becomes != latest, restore from `pg.turns[viewTurn]` into `pg` for rendering. When it returns to latest, restore the live state.

I'll implement state-swap: maintain `pg._liveSnap` to preserve current live state when entering past view. On any nav action, recompute by restoring live first (if needed), then if not latest, swap to snapshot for render.

{
  "type": "tool_use",
  "id": "toolu_016YNdEnE3v6pZt6dKRECohY",
  "name": "Edit",
  "input": {
    "replace_all": false,

    "old_string": "// Turn-navigation \"Next >\" button — only scrubs the slider forward through past turns.\n// Ending a player's turn now lives in pgConfirmEndTurn.\nfunction pgNextTurn() {\n  if (pg.phase !== \"play\") return;\n  if (!pgIsViewingLatest()) {\n    pg.viewTurn += 1;\n    pgRenderTurnNav();\n    return;\n  }\n  pgStatus(\"Already at the latest turn. Use Confirm End Turn to end the current player's turn.\", \"info\");\n}",
    "new_string": "// Turn-navigation \"Next >\" button — only scrubs the slider forward through past turns.\n// Ending a player's turn now lives in pgConfirmEndTurn.\nfunction pgNextTurn() {\n  if (pg.phase !== \"play\") return;\n  if (!pgIsViewingLatest()) {\n    pg.viewTurn += 1;\n    pgApplyView();\n    return;\n  }\n  pgStatus(\"Already at the latest turn. Use Confirm End Turn to end the current player's turn.\", \"info\");\n}\n\n// Apply pg.viewTurn: if past, swap in the snapshot's state for rendering;\n// if back to latest, restore the live state we stashed.\nfunction pgApplyView() {\n  const max = pg.turns.length - 1;\n  if (pg.viewTurn > max) pg.viewTurn = max;\n  if (pg.viewTurn < 0) pg.viewTurn = 0;\n  const viewingPast = pg.viewTurn < max;\n  if (viewingPast) {\n    // Stash live state once when first leaving \"latest\".\n    if (!pg._liveSnap) {\n      pg._liveSnap = {\n        currentTurnIdx: pg.currentTurnIdx,\n        players: pgClone(pg.players),\n        bank: { ...pg.bank },\n        bankDevCards: pg.bankDevCards,\n        settlements: pgClone(pg.settlements),\n        roads: pgClone(pg.roads),\n        robberHex: pg.robberHex,\n        eventLog: pgClone(pg.eventLog),\n        rollHistory: pgClone(pg.rollHistory),\n      };\n    }\n    const snap = pg.turns[pg.viewTurn];\n    if (snap) {\n      pg.currentTurnIdx = snap.currentTurnIdx;\n      pg.players = pgClone(snap.players);\n      pg.bank = { ...snap.bank };\n      pg.bankDevCards = snap.bankDevCards;\n      pg.settlements = pgClone(snap.settlements);\n      pg.roads = pgClone(snap.roads);\n      pg.robberHex = snap.robberHex;\n      pg.eventLog = pg._liveSnap.eventLog.slice(0, snap.eventLogLen);\n      pg.rollHistory = pg._liveSnap.rollHistory.slice(0, snap.rollLen);\n    }\n  } else if (pg._liveSnap) {\n    // Returning to latest — restore live state.\n    pg.currentTurnIdx = pg._liveSnap.currentTurnIdx;\n    pg.players = pg._liveSnap.players;\n    pg.bank = pg._liveSnap.bank;\n    pg.bankDevCards = pg._liveSnap.bankDevCards;\n    pg.settlements = pg._liveSnap.settlements;\n    pg.roads = pg._liveSnap.roads;\n    pg.robberHex = pg._liveSnap.robberHex;\n    pg.eventLog = pg._liveSnap.eventLog;\n    pg.rollHistory = pg._liveSnap.rollHistory;\n    pg._liveSnap = null;\n  }\n  pgRenderAll();\n}"
  },
  "caller": {
    "type": "direct"
  }
}

Now I need to also handle a critical edge: when a new game action happens (`pgSnapshotTurn` is called), if `pg._liveSnap` exists (meaning we were viewing past), we should NOT take a snapshot from displayed state but from live state. But `pgRequireLatestView` already blocks user actions in past view via `pgIsViewingLatest()`. Still, I should ensure that path forces back-to-latest before any change... Actually look at `pgIsViewingLatest`:

```js
function pgIsViewingLatest() {
  return pg.viewTurn >= pg.turns.length - 1;
}
```

If we're in past view (`viewTurn < pg.turns.length - 1`), this returns false, so any action calling `pgRequireLatestView` is blocked. Good. So the user can't take any new actions while in past view, meaning `pg.players` etc. won't be mutated as snapshot state. 

But there's still one issue: undo/redo. `pgUndo` does `Object.assign(pg, prev.snap)`, which restores all fields including `viewTurn` and `turns`. After undo, `_liveSnap` would still exist from before, which is wrong. Let me check `pgCaptureSnap` — it doesn't capture `_liveSnap`, so after undo, `pg._liveSnap` would remain stale. All three bugs fixed.

**Summary:**

1. **Analytics game-end player names** ([js/script.js:1695-1700](js/script.js#L1695-L1700)): `analyticsGetPlayerName` was indexing `playerUserStates` (an array, not a map) by color id and falling back to `Player ${colorId}`. Now maps the color id through `playOrder` to render `Player 1/2/3/4` by turn order, matching the analytics player tracker.

2. **Dice distribution y-axis padding** ([js/script.js:934](js/script.js#L934)): Increased the analytics dice chart's `margin.left` from 30 to 40, giving the rotated "Frequency" label ~10px more breathing room from the y-axis ticks.

3. **Playground turn navigator** ([js/script.js:4828-4876](js/script.js#L4828-L4876)): Added `pgApplyView()` that swaps the snapshot stored in `pg.turns[viewTurn]` (players, bank, settlements, roads, robber, sliced event log/rollHistory) into `pg` for rendering, stashing the live state in `pg._liveSnap` so it's restored when scrubbing back to latest. Wired `pgPrevTurnBtn`, `pgTurnSlider`, and `pgNextTurn` to call `pgApplyView()` instead of just `pgRenderTurnNav()`. Cleared `_liveSnap` in undo/redo and reset paths so they don't carry stale state.

    "old_string": "function pgManualAdjust(kind, delta) {\n  if (!pgRequireLatestView(\"adjusting resources\")) return;\n  if (pgBlockedByPending(\"adjusting resources\")) return;\n  const playerId = Number(document.getElementById(\"playerSelector\")?.value);\n  const player = pg.players.find(p => p.id === playerId);\n  if (!player) return;\n  const typeId = kind === \"resources\" ? \"resourceType\" : \"devCardType\";\n  const amountId = kind === \"resources\" ? \"resourceAmount\" : \"devCardAmount\";\n  const cardType = document.getElementById(typeId)?.value;\n  const amt = Number(document.getElementById(amountId)?.value || 1);\n  if (!cardType || !Number.isFinite(amt) || amt <= 0) return;\n  pgPushHistory(`manual-${kind}`);\n  player[kind][cardType] = Math.max(0, (player[kind][cardType] || 0) + delta * amt);\n  if (kind === \"resources\") {\n    const t = PG_RES_NAME_TO_TYPE[cardType];\n    if (t) pg.bank[t] = Math.max(0, pg.bank[t] - delta * amt);\n  }\n  pgRenderAll();\n}\n\n",

  },
  "caller": {
    "type": "direct"
  }
}

```json
{
  "type": "tool_use",
  "id": "toolu_01GUYwSTo1UpMvgq2Doan9gx",
  "name": "Edit",
  "input": {
    "replace_all": false,
    "file_path": "/home/kai/cmsc471-final/js/script.js",
    "old_string": "  diceRollChart = {\n    diceValues,\n    colorScale,\n    innerHeight,\n    x,\n    yAxisGroup,\n    xAxisLabel,\n    yAxisLabel,\n    barsGroup,\n    labelsGroup\n  };\n\n  updateDiceRollChart();\n}\n\nfunction updateDiceRollChart() {\n  if (!diceRollChart) {\n    initDiceRollChart();\n    if (!diceRollChart) return;\n  }\n\n  const data = diceRollChart.diceValues.map((value) => ({\n    value,\n    count: (typeof pg !== \"undefined\" && Array.isArray(pg.rollHistory) ? pg.rollHistory : playgroundState.rollHistory).reduce((total, entry) => total + (entry.value === value ? 1 : 0), 0)\n  }));\n\n  const maxCount = d3.max(data, (d) => d.count) ?? 0;\n  const yMax = Math.max(1, maxCount);\n  const y = d3.scaleLinear()\n    .domain([0, yMax])\n    .range([diceRollChart.innerHeight, 0]);\n\n  // Only show ticks at the distinct bar heights (plus 0 and the max). Keeps the axis clean\n  // when most bars share the same count.\n  const distinctCounts = Array.from(new Set([0, yMax, ...data.map((d) => d.count)])).sort((a, b) => a - b);\n  diceRollChart.yAxisGroup\n    .call(\n      d3.axisLeft(y)\n        .tickValues(distinctCounts)\n        .tickFormat(d3.format(\"d\"))\n    );\n\n  diceRollChart.yAxisGroup.selectAll(\"text\")\n    .style(\"fill\", \"#cbd5e1\")\n    .style(\"font-size\", \"11px\");\n  diceRollChart.yAxisGroup.selectAll(\"path, line\")\n    .style(\"stroke\", \"#64748b\");\n\n  const bars = diceRollChart.barsGroup.selectAll(\"rect\").data(data, (d) => d.value);\n\n  bars.join(\"rect\")\n    .attr(\"x\", (d) => diceRollChart.x(d.value))\n    .attr(\"y\", (d) => y(d.count))\n    .attr(\"width\", diceRollChart.x.bandwidth())\n    .attr(\"height\", (d) => diceRollChart.innerHeight - y(d.count))\n    .attr(\"fill\", (d) => diceRollChart.colorScale(d.value));\n\n  const labels = diceRollChart.labelsGroup.selectAll(\"text\").data(data, (d) => d.value);\n\n  labels.join(\"text\")\n    .attr(\"x\", (d) => (diceRollChart.x(d.value) ?? 0) + diceRollChart.x.bandwidth() / 2)\n    .attr(\"y\", (d) => (d.count > 0 ? y(d.count) - 6 : y(0) - 6))\n    .attr(\"text-anchor\", \"middle\")\n    .style(\"fill\", \"#e2e8f0\")\n    .style(\"font-size\", \"11px\")\n    .style(\"font-weight\", \"600\")\n    .text((d) => d.count);\n}",
    "new_string": "  diceRollChart = {\n    diceValues,\n    colorScale,\n    innerHeight,\n    x,\n    yAxisGroup,\n    xAxisLabel,\n    yAxisLabel,\n    barsGroup,\n    labelsGroup,\n    barTransition: () => d3.transition().duration(450).ease(d3.easeCubicOut)\n  };\n\n  updateDiceRollChart();\n}\n\nfunction updateDiceRollChart() {\n  if (!diceRollChart) {\n    initDiceRollChart();\n    if (!diceRollChart) return;\n  }\n\n  const { diceValues, colorScale, innerHeight, x, yAxisGroup, barsGroup, labelsGroup, barTransition } = diceRollChart;\n\n  const rollHistory = (typeof pg !== \"undefined\" && Array.isArray(pg.rollHistory) ? pg.rollHistory : playgroundState.rollHistory);\n  const data = diceValues.map((value) => ({\n    value,\n    count: rollHistory.reduce((total, entry) => total + (entry.value === value ? 1 : 0), 0)\n  }));\n\n  const maxCount = d3.max(data, (d) => d.count) ?? 0;\n  const yMax = Math.max(1, maxCount);\n  const y = d3.scaleLinear()\n    .domain([0, yMax])\n    .range([innerHeight, 0]);\n\n  yAxisGroup.interrupt();\n  barsGroup.selectAll(\"rect\").interrupt();\n  labelsGroup.selectAll(\"text\").interrupt();\n\n  const t = barTransition();\n\n  // Only show ticks at the distinct bar heights (plus 0 and the max). Keeps the axis clean\n  // when most bars share the same count.\n  const distinctCounts = Array.from(new Set([0, yMax, ...data.map((d) => d.count)])).sort((a, b) => a - b);\n  yAxisGroup\n    .transition(t)\n    .call(\n      d3.axisLeft(y)\n        .tickValues(distinctCounts)\n        .tickFormat(d3.format(\"d\"))\n    );\n\n  yAxisGroup.selectAll(\"text\")\n    .style(\"fill\", \"#cbd5e1\")\n    .style(\"font-size\", \"11px\");\n  yAxisGroup.selectAll(\"path, line\")\n    .style(\"stroke\", \"#64748b\");\n\n  const rect = barsGroup.selectAll(\"rect\").data(data, (d) => d.value);\n\n  const rectEnter = rect.enter()\n    .append(\"rect\")\n    .attr(\"x\", (d) => x(d.value))\n    .attr(\"width\", x.bandwidth())\n    .attr(\"y\", innerHeight)\n    .attr(\"height\", 0)\n    .attr(\"fill\", (d) => colorScale(d.value));\n\n  rect.merge(rectEnter)\n    .transition(t)\n    .attr(\"x\", (d) => x(d.value))\n    .attr(\"width\", x.bandwidth())\n    .attr(\"y\", (d) => y(d.count))\n    .attr(\"height\", (d) => innerHeight - y(d.count))\n    .attr(\"fill\", (d) => colorScale(d.value));\n\n  rect.exit().remove();\n\n  const texts = labelsGroup.selectAll(\"text\").data(data, (d) => d.value);\n\n  const textsEnter = texts.enter()\n    .append(\"text\")\n    .attr(\"text-anchor\", \"middle\")\n    .style(\"fill\", \"#e2e8f0\")\n    .style(\"font-size\", \"11px\")\n    .style(\"font-weight\", \"600\");\n\n  texts.merge(textsEnter)\n    .transition(t)\n    .attr(\"x\", (d) => (x(d.value) ?? 0) + x.bandwidth() / 2)\n    .attr(\"y\", (d) => (d.count > 0 ? y(d.count) - 6 : y(0) - 6))\n    .text((d) => d.count);\n\n  texts.exit().remove();\n}"
  },
  "caller": {
    "type": "direct"
  }
}
```

The playground dice chart now uses the same 450ms `easeCubicOut` transition as the analytics chart — bars enter rising from the x-axis (height 0 at y=innerHeight, growing up to their final height), bars/labels/y-axis smoothly transition on every update, and prior transitions are interrupted to avoid stacking.
